items.title: Szoboszlai: 'I did my homework' on trick free kick
items.summary: Dominik Szoboszlai said he did his "homework" paid off on his trick free kick that gave Liverpool the lead in a 3-0 win at Marseille in the Champions League on Wednesday.

items.title: Barça win, but injury for Pedri 'not good news'
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said it's "not good news" after losing Pedri to a hamstring injury in Wednesday's 4-2 Champions League win away at Slavia Prague.

items.title: Madrid tops Money League as teams clear $12B
items.summary: Real Madrid topped the latest Deloitte Football Money League rankings, generating a record €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) in revenue, with the top 20 teams represented clearing €12.4 billion ($14.5 billion) when combined, also a record.

items.title: How to reach the Champions League knockouts: Can Chelsea, Barça avoid the playoff round?
items.summary: We're less than a week away from knowing which 24 teams advance from the league phase. Here's what's at stake with only one matchweek to go.

items.title: If Liverpool want to be successful this season, all roads lead through Szoboszlai
items.summary: Liverpool cruised to a convincing 3-0 victory over Marseille, aided by an excellent performance from Dominik Szoboszlai.

items.title: 'He came here for these nights' - Wissa inspires Newcastle
items.summary: Eddie Howe says Yoane Wissa came to Newcastle "for these nights" after his first Champions League goal at St James' Park.

items.title: Slot praises Salah return on important night for both
items.summary: Arne Slot praises the return of Mohamed Salah, with Liverpool's Champions League win in Marseille proving important for both.

items.title: Liverpool rise up football rich list but Premier League shut out of top four
items.summary: Reds’ success aided by Taylor Swift and Dua Lipa gigs Real Madrid take top spot again with £1.4bn revenue Liverpool were the English club with the highest revenue last season according to the annual Deloitte Football Money League – but for the first time in the report’s 29-year history no Premier League club made the top four. Real Madrid again took top spot with €1.61bn (£1.4bn), far ahead of Barcelona, with €974m. Bayern Munich with €860m and Paris Saint-Germain with €837m were third and fourth respectively. Continue reading...

items.title: Liverpool top English club in Deloitte Money League
items.summary: Liverpool become the top-earning Premier League club for the first time, according to analysis from financial firm Deloitte.

items.title: Who can join Arsenal & Bayern in Champions League knockouts?
items.summary: With the league phase of Champions League almost at its conclusion, which teams can join Arsenal and Bayern Munich in the top eight?

items.title: PSV gaat als 22e de laatste wedstrijd van de competitiefase van de CL in
items.summary: PSV stuitte woensdag op Newcastle United in speelronde zeven van de competitiefase van de Champions League. In deze VI Live miste je niets van dat belangrijke duel, dat eindigde in mineur.

items.title: Engelsen schrikken van PSV: 'Gedoemd om in de problemen te komen'
items.summary: De afgang van PSV in Newcastle maakt ook in Engeland de tongen los. Op het Britse eiland zijn ze enigszins geschrokken van de Eindhovenaren, die eerder dit seizoen nog zo'n goede indruk achterlieten.

items.title: PSV in cijfers: foutenfestival en minste schoten onder Bosz
items.summary: Het Champions League-duel met Newcastle United is woensdagavond voor PSV uitgedraaid op een deceptie. De Eindhovenaren kwamen snel op achterstand, hadden geen moment zicht op een resultaat en verloren uiteindelijk met ruime cijfers: 3-0. Dit zijn de belangrijkste statistieken uit de wedstrijd, waarin PSV nauwelijks gevaar wist te stichten en vooral zichzelf in de problemen bracht.

items.title: Zenden en Janssen discussiëren over kaart Guimarães: 'Voor mij was het rood'
items.summary: Boudewijn Zenden snapte niet dat Newcastle United de wedstrijd met elf man uitspeelde. Aanvoerder Bruno Guimarães plantte in de eerste helft zijn noppen op de enkel van Joey Veerman. Hij kwam weg met een gele kaart en dat zorgde voor ongeloof bij de analyticus van Ziggo Sport. Theo Janssen vond het geen rood.

items.title: Bosz wil niets weten van 'afgetroefd' PSV: 'Nee, dat vind ik totaal niet'
items.summary: Peter Bosz wil er niets van weten dat PSV afgetroefd zou zijn door Newcastle United in de Champions League. Woensdagavond verloren de Eindhovenaren in Engeland met ruime cijfers en bovendien creëerden ze zelf nauwelijks kansen.

items.title: Papers: Palmer keen on potential Man Utd transfer
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Thursday's newspapers...

items.title: Salah returns as Liverpool score three to beat Marseille
items.summary: Mohammad Salah returns to the Liverpool starting XI as Liverpool score three goals, including an intelligent Dominik Szoboszlai free kick, to beat Olympique Marseille.

items.title: Szoboszlai’s low blow puts Liverpool on a high as Salah returns in Marseille
items.summary: The intense, intimidating examination by Roberto De Zerbi’s Marseille never materialised. Liverpool, again more comfortable on European soil and without the irritation of facing a low block, delivered a ­commanding display to advance on automatic qualification for the last 16 and leave their Premier League troubles behind. A Dominik Szoboszlai free‑kick, an own goal forced by Jeremie ­Frimpong and a stoppage-time strike from ­the substitute Cody Gakpo gave Arne Slot’s side a merited victory in the south of France. Continue reading...

items.title: Liverpool close on CL knockouts with big win over Marseille
items.summary: Liverpool moved up to fourth in the Champions League table with an impressive 3-0 win over Marseille at the Stade Velodrome.

items.title: Barnes seals easy win against PSV but Newcastle face wait over Guimarães
items.summary: There are many ways to self-destruct on a football pitch but PSV ­Eindhoven chose one of the more obvious methods. In opting to play out slowly from the back against high-pressing opponents possessing forwards blessed with the speed and skill of Yoane Wissa and Anthony Gordon, Peter Bosz’s team were always likely to come undone. Continue reading...
